subject: Project Management
title: The Japanese Influence
description: A fast breakdown of The Japanese Influence for builders.

hook.text: In 1986, two professors studied how Honda and Toyota developed products — fast and flexibly.

buildup.text: Takeuchi and Nonaka published 'The New New Product Development Game' in Harvard Business Review.

discovery.text: They described small, cross-functional teams that worked in overlapping phases, not sequences.

twist.text: They compared these teams to a rugby scrum — a word that would later name an entire framework.

climax.text: A paper about Japanese manufacturing would eventually reshape the entire software industry.

punchline.text: Software's future was born on a Japanese factory floor.

quiz.question: What metaphor did Takeuchi and Nonaka use for cross-functional teams?
